reviews.review_description: It’s not hyperbole to say this is my favorite Quest app yet. I spend hours playing these puzzles and think about them when I’m not. I surprisingly still have more puzzles to go but there is replayability too. The music is charming and it’s great with mixed reality. Really I have no notes.
I hate the fact that this game destroyed my intelligence and embarrassed me Infront of my friends, but, it's still pretty good

Cubism delivers calming yet challenging 3D puzzles in VR. With intuitive interactions, clean visuals, and soothing music, it’s perfect for players who love spatial thinking and mindful gameplay. A standout title on Meta Quest.
￼ Why I go it with horizon plus and then it’s just a puzzle game and I don’t like puzzle games

developer_response.body:
Hey mrpumpkinvryoutube, thank you for your honest review. Puzzle games aren't everyone's cup of tea, and that's ok! I try and make sure it's pretty clear what this game is and what it isn't, and if there's anything about its presentation that made you feel it wasn't a straight-up puzzle game, feel free to let me know at thomas@cubism-vr.com!

If you still have your Horizon+ subscription, make sure to check out the other games in the catalogue, they're not all puzzle games and I'm sure there are some that you would like!

reviews.review_description: Fun game for everyone, especially great for newcomers. With hand tracking and mixed reality this game is something extraordinary - really tricks your mind into thinking there are real floating cubes in your room. Truly a unique experience.

This game was enjoyable to me and also my family. This game is calming and also fun. I play this in my free time with no kids in the house and just myself. It makes me feel happy inside. This is your chance now to get cubism to have fun with not only your family but yourself as well!!!!
I love it
Very good puzzle game, I really like the mixed reality and hand tracking features, making it feel like there’s actually floating cubes in your living room!

My Grandfather is 72years old. Recently, my grandmother moved on. I’ve been going over once or twice a week and spending quality time with him. He’s a very sweet, easygoing, old hippie with a big white beard. One day, I had the bright idea to bring over my new Quest 3s to surprise him with cool new video game hardware he’s never seen before. For reference this man was alive when Pac-Man required an entire cabinet of cables. I put on cubism, handed him the headset with no controllers, and told him to have fun! Immediately after I look down to find a slimy, brown substance going down my grandfathers leg. Pants were ruined but memories were made. Coolest game he’s ever played, 5STARS

reviews.review_title: Becoming a classic MR puzzle game...
reviews.review_description:
hard not to recommend this one to newer users to vr or those that just want a laid back experience where they can do some 3d puzzles in away only vr can do...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
It’s ok I guess
This is by far the best quality vr puzzle game I ever played
Super clean and well done!

Cubism is a deceptively simple yet deeply satisfying 3D puzzle game. The concept sounds easy enough — fit all the pieces together to form a specific shape — but the execution quickly proves otherwise. As the number of pieces increases, so does the complexity, and what starts as a calm exercise in logic can soon feel like an elegant battle of wits.

Some puzzles seem unsolvable at first, looping you into trying the same configurations until you finally think outside the box. There’s no timer, no pressure — just a peaceful atmosphere, soothing music, and the quiet satisfaction of finding the perfect fit. A cozy, cerebral gem for anyone who loves brain-teasing challenges in VR.

⭐️ 5/5 – Welcome to Puzzle City.

reviews.review_title: Can't play the game: not detected
reviews.review_description:
I literally just bought the meta quest 3 to play this game, but when I open the game and it starts loading, a message shows up "controllers not found make sure they are tracking correctly". I've been using the meta quest with another games and apps and the hand tracking and controllers are working just fine. 

I tried re-installing the game, rebooting the device, synchronizing the controllers again but nothing works. I'm dying to play this game, can anyone help me to fix this?

UPDATE: A factory reset to my device solved the problem as Thomas suggested. Thank you very much for your assistance! The game is fun af!

developer_response.body:
Hi Chuysin, thank you for reaching out, and sorry to hear you're having this issue! 
To be honest, it's the first time I've heard of this issue - but I'll see what I can do to figure out what might be going on. 
For starters, could you let me know what firmware version your Quest is on? Are there any other apps or settings enabled that could be affecting the game, such as Quest games optimizer? 
Feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com and I'll be happy to personally follow up with you there!

reviews.review_description: I love this game. The physics feel like real life. I love how I can use my hands and easily move the blocks in space and throw them and view the container from any angle. It's really fun and just blows my mind with the power of VR/AR. Definitely recommend!

Great graphical and audio design creates a wonderful presentation in both vr and ar, controls and hand tracking are top-notch, and most importantly the puzzles are quite challenging, overall 10\10 I highly recommend it

I note a previous review from several days ago had same issue I'm having, that when I attempt to launch the game, a message states controllers not detected and hand tracking not recognised. All other games work just fine. I note the solution was to do a factory reset on the headset, which clearly I don't want to do as I'll lose everything. A shame, as it looks from all the reviews to be an excellent puzzle game
UPDATE - developer has reached out to provide solution with some excellent feedback, a big thumbs up from me

developer_response.body:
Hey Martyn, thank you for flagging this issue. My best guess from the last time this happened is that this is related to a controller firmware, as the factory reset the other user tried would have forced their controllers to update. I totally understand you not wanting to factory reset your device though.
If it is indeed a firmware issue its not something I can do something about sadly, but I did ping my contacts at Meta again to see if they can provide more clarity on what might be going on. Please reach out to me at thomas@cubism-vr.com, and I'll be happy to follow up with you when I learn more about what might be causing this issue!

reviews.review_description: I removed my frustrated review and will update properly soon.. but for time being.. and have increased my stars due to figuring out that I can turn down the beeping sound in settings that begins in level 2 when pieces are positioned incorrectly. I would just like to respond to the developer Thomas’ reply and ask why it cannot be made to have the puzzle pieces click into place only when they are correctly positioned? Seems like an easy fix and then there would not be a need for the annoying beeping that I personally find highly jarring to the central nervous system. Perhaps some might enjoy it? Hmm okay well thank you for making this game and providing the option to turn the beeping all the way down and the music on continuous (otherwise the music goes away along with the beeping.) Now I can fully enjoy!!

developer_response.body:
Update: Hey Jjaye, just wanted to let you know that I've added an option to mute this sound, which will be included in the next update! (which should normally be going out next month)
On your follow up question - puzzles can have multiple solutions, so only snapping them when they're right might be a bit tricky. Also I think many folks like thinking through solutions by placing the pieces in the grid.
Thanks again for your feedback, and hope you enjoy next months update!

Hey JJaye,
Thank you for sharing your honest review. I added the pinging sound because playtesting during development showed people would often misplace pieces and not realize it, thinking they had solved the puzzle, but not realizing a piece was sticking out in the back. This is why the sound is there, and also why I made it slightly annoying so it's hard to miss - because what I found from playtesting is that for most people it can be much more annoying to falsely think you've finished a puzzle and not have anything happen in the game.
Very much appreciate your opinion on this though - perhaps I could add a setting to turn that sound off in the near future.
- Thomas

reviews.review_description:
I usually avoid puzzle games because I find them frustrating, but my wife downloaded this game and I thought it was a great.  

Puzzles obviously get increasingly more difficult but it's a very well presented and nearly packaged experience.

its amazing i really am addicted to it but i wish there were hints to solve puzzle
but there are no bugs at all. Amazing game

This game is one of the BEST games included with horizon+ and just by installing it, I was hooked! Its background music makes it relaxing play this app. And I also like the nice cube connecting it’s the perfect game!
Simultaneously the most calming and challenging game I play on Quest. Highly, highly recommend! 5/5!
A simple little puzzle game that is very relaxing. Would recommend to anyone who wants to escape with a chill puzzle.

One of my favourite things in VR is the hand tracking but some games never seem to get it right, but this one just gets everything correct. the finer details like when a block is too far away so you pretend to grab it and pull it back towards you, and it just comes back. I am so grateful for the developers of this game because it shows how a hand tracking game can perform and how everything is tracked in a developer mode. keep up the great work lads, can’t wait to see yer next projects!!!

reviews.review_title: Finally brain exercise for all ages
reviews.review_description: Someday when you don’t feel like shooting zombies or test you archery skills against the kings men, and just want to sit down relax and take you time- this game is perfect and might I add very appropriate for all ages
i enjoyed my playthrough and I loved how the music interacts with the game!

I have played through about half the Volume 1 puzzles and am really enjoying this game so far. It's just a peaceful little puzzle to solve, low pressure with sitting classical music in the background. Relaxing but the puzzles get pretty tough as the shapes get more complex. It sort of feels like playing in space the way the pieces float around.

Cubism is the most elegant puzzle game on the quest store.
Highly recommended for people who love puzzles and anyone with a quest should try it to see a great example of what a made game looks like. I love the level of detail that was put into every button, poke, and pinch it makes the virtual blocks feel real

This game is awesome. Easy and challenging puzzles and don’t even get me started on the level editor, it can be activated in the general settings in game by the way, creating and playing levels are SO fun, sure I can get mad at it, but that’s when the puzzle is way too hard. Oh and devs, add hints to the game maybe, that would be nice, because sometimes the game can get too hard. When it does, I suggest taking at least a 25 minute break and calm down a little bit, works for me!

reviews.review_title: A great break from shooting things
reviews.review_description: Incredibly relaxing and a great brain exercise. It's a great break from the chaos of BoneLab and SUPERHOT. Most fun I've had in a puzzle game since Portal

Really enjoying the game so far. Has a very minimalist, calming effect while being engaging enough to work your brain in the right way. Controls are excellent and work very precisely. I hope the developer might have more levels in store or a DLC pack we can purchase some day. I’m only a few levels in at this point but I know I will be wanting more. Thank you for making this game, and keeping the price reasonable (got it on sale for $4.99)!

I love this game - simple but beautifully designed. To me, this is a perfect use of VR. Move floating 3D shapes around to complete the puzzle while listening to soothing music. No time pressure but the puzzles get harder! For such a simple game, I think the visuals are just lovely too.
Great way to test hand tracking and nice meditative puzzles!

I love playing this game in passthrough (MR). I can sit in bed, watch TV or listen to an audiobook, and challenge my mind. I love throwing the pieces across the room and watching them float back. Would like a wider variety of the soothing music.
Game is very relaxing and great with the pass through on the quest 2
Wow!! This is just awesome!! You should buy this!
Great game. it makes me really use my brain.  That is a good thing at my age! I feel challenged enough to solve the puzzles.  I wish they would make more levels!!? I hope, I hope, I hope!!!!
Guys Should I Play It? Just got a new quest 3S today
good as it should

I discovered this game with my free 3-month subscription to meta+ and had never heard about it.
My expectations were quite low because I have too often been underwhelmed by VR games.

But I love it.
The simple but pleasing sleek aesthetic of the game paired with the quiet intermittent music that somehow reminded me of wandering around Hyrule in Zelda BOTW's more desolate moments provide for a general feeling of serene solitude which is becoming all but too rare in today's world of constant overstimulation and stress. It is so nice to be able to slow down the pace, breathe and just savor the moment.
The sensory experience of handling the pieces and the box, letting the pieces float around you, catching them and fitting them into the box is extremely satisfying.

Feeling the tension build up when you are stumbling on a puzzle and then finding the solution is also quite pleasing.

All in all, Cubism is a game that makes you feel good.

When I really like a game, I like to check the negative reviews because I want to know what people with another point of view might think.
From what I see in the comments, the two main categories of people who publish a negative review of Cubism are contrarians, who can't stand a consensual score  of 5 for a game which they were not overly impressed by, and people who are so used to constantly being overstimulated that they get bored or actually get stressed when they experience solitude.
I was glad to see how involved Thomas was in addressing their criticisms with tact and dedidication to improving his own game.

I do have one gripe though... As the game is part of my meta quest+ subscription, I am unable to buy it so I can have it in my collection and support a dev who clearly deserves it.

Love the game, many challenges and great music, very relaxing and brings me back to play the next day.

Highly recommended!!

developer_response.body:
Hi ramonmata,
Thank you for your kind review! I'd love to look in to the bug you mentioned - at what points in the game does the menu open by itself? This should only happen right after you finish a puzzle. Feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com and I'd be happy to follow up on this!

reviews.review_description: This is a beautifuly made puzzle game , it shows you the ropes for a while then pulls the rug out from under you and lets you sink or swim , but when you finish the puzzles what a feeling of accomplishment

reviews.review_title: One of the best puzzle game on vr
reviews.review_description: I love the idea of puzzles in mixed reality, and this game did just that. Floating blocks across my room while I'm trying to solve a shape is actually fun.The piano songs are so relaxing and give a vibe of something elegant. The game is amazing and all but I really wish they'd add a block blast type mini-game, I'd play this 24/7

While most Virtual Reality games emphasize the immersive nature of the medium, few handle the ability to manipulate virtual objects freely in 3D space. While there are certainly puzzle games in VR (like Tetris Effect), none quite scratch that IQ test itch like Cubism. Of course, with puzzle games like this, there comes a point where the game gets too challenging to complete. This is even without a timer or other mechanic trying to pressure the player to work faster. Still, Cubism is a relaxing challenge for your mind.

For those new to Virtual Reality, Cubism is a good primer for how to use the controllers (or even the hand-tracking features). Since the entire game is merely manipulating different shapes to fit into a 3D outlined space, there’s a lot of grabbing, rotating, and placing. It can be played seated, and doesn’t have any extreme elements that might induce motion sickness. This game could easily be ported to other non-VR platforms, but I think it works best as a VR game because it fully uses the 3D space by having the player physically move the pieces to where they need to go.

The simplicity of Cubism is part of its charm. It’s just you and the pieces—and soothing piano music by Robert Schumann. Trying to work out mentally how these pieces fit together is sometimes just a matter of trial and error. However, there are later puzzles at the higher difficulties where certain wrong solutions get stuck in your mind and it would be helpful if there was a hint system of some kind to push past these mental blocks. As it is, I could not fully complete the game, but I definitely got a lot of enjoyment out of it while it lasted.

A simple but gradually challenging puzzle game, I give Cubism 4.0 stars out of 5.
Saw it with my free meta+.  Downloaded it and it's great.  The AR component is awesome.

reviews.review_title: Best VR/XR Puzzle Game on Quest!
reviews.review_description:
Returning to edit my review since it’s been a while.

Still love the game! Perfect for playing a challenging game that helps build memory and motion skills.

The dynamic control scheme helps a ton when using hand tracking since you can grab blocks that are far  away and move the entire puzzle around with simple gestures.

Hand tracking has improved a lot with more updates, and the ability to physically press buttons feels so pristine compared to normal point and click UI.

I strongly recommend this game, even if you aren’t too great at puzzles! It helps build so many skills and ramps up slowly to provide a fitting experience to all.

Thanks developers for the response!
Super fun to sit and play, my wife loves it too.

So this is crazy but I found this game after finishing the meta quest hand tracking tutorial. It was exactly 5 bucks which is what I had leftover from a quest gift card. I bought the game and id say it’s a decent 9/10. I feel like hints should be implemented since some levels are definitely very tough and there should be a way to get help. But other than that, it’s a  good game!

reviews.review_title: A good puzzle; wish it was more…
reviews.review_description: Challenging, good graphics, but I wish there was hand control.

developer_response.body:
Hey GeeZane, there actually is full hand tracking support! Normally, you should just be able to double tap your controllers, and Quest will switch to hand tracking in the game. (Make sure hand tracking is enabled in the Quest settings menu first though)

Let me know if its working for you, and if not feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com if you need further support!

reviews.review_description: Without hints this game is so hard I think this game needs hints.

developer_response.body:
Hey Xaiden67, thank you for your honest feedback. I designed the game so you can easily skip levels if you're stuck - in fact, you can unlock all puzzles by solving only the 'easy' and 'normal' difficulty puzzles! 
If you're still stuck, there are actually a few youtube videos going over all solutions - just search "Cubism solutions" on youtube and they should show up.

If you'd rather have a hint instead of the whole solution on any specific puzzle, feel free to shoot me an e-mail directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com!

reviews.review_description: I consider this a must-buy for your VR library. This game is such a pleasant and smooth experience. The puzzles are really well designed and they range from easy to hard, but never boring or impossible. This game is perfect, buy it.
It’s so realaxing and fun but there is a creepy music when we put the puzzle wrong

The puzzles are good. The ability to play in pass-thru mode is good (Q3). I feel the difficulty of each puzzle has something to do with the way you orient the grid area, and how you start each puzzle. Sometimes the puzzles labeled HARD are easier than the ones labeled NORMAL (medium?). Overall a very good vr experience for me (60M).
If u aw stoopid don t by dis gam or els u won t complet any pussels and won t lik it

developer_response.body: Hey jxpollard, thanks for your review, and sorry to hear you're finding some of the puzzles difficult! If you want a hint on some of the levels, search "Cubism (VR) Solutions" on youtube and check out Sonny Li's video where he shows all the solutions - it's a great way to get a quick hint on a puzzle you may be stuck on :)

reviews.review_description:
Getting disconnected. I pick or touch a piece of a puzzle, hands turn red and I can’t continue. 
Blah.

developer_response.body:
Hey Chris,
Thank you for your feedback and sorry to hear you're having issues with hand tracking. If your hands are turning red often there may be an issue with your tracking, possibly related to the conditions you're playing in. The most important way to ensure better handtracking is playing in a well lit room, ideally by day-light. 
If you continue to experience tracking issues, please feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com, and I'll be happy to assist you further!

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games.
reviews.review_description:
As a puzzle game... IT'S AWESOME.

Gives you the opportunity to explore the potential of MR and it's doing it's job very well. 

One of the best €10 that one can spend!

Bad controls
Update 1:  Still boring and bad controls.
Update 2:  Still boring and bad controls.

developer_response.body: Hey Jerry, sorry to hear you're still having issues with the controls. Many improvements have been made since the release of the game which have made it more accessible to most players, but it will never be perfect for everyone, so no hard feelings if this game isn't for you.

reviews.review_description:
I got this game for the hand tracking, and found out 3D puzzles are actually super fun! It works pretty well. I felt like I was Tony Stark rotating shapes in the air - ok maybe its the Windows95 version of Jarvis. Not as fluid as the movies but still really fun!

My one complaint is it only uses your thumb and index finger to grab things. To pick up some of the larger pieces my natural instinct is always to use my middle finger which just passes through the object. If my middle finger worked I'd give it the full 5 stars.

Regarding the puzzles: The easy/normal difficulty usually takes no more than a couple minutes. However, I've run into harder difficulty that take a bit more thought and take me 15min or so. I enjoy the challenge. I think I have about half the puzzles left, many of them higher difficulty.

I got sucked in my first time playing and went for over 2 hours. The music is very peaceful and combined with the 3D puzzles it was just a fantastic way to wind down.

I suspect i have several more hours to go to complete all the puzzles - great value at $10. Thank you devs!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review and your feedback, obi2kenobi!

I understand where you're coming from regarding grabbing with the middle finger - when I first started implementing hand tracking, I actually tried supporting both middle and index finger for grabbing. However, during early playtests I noticed that this led to a lot of unintentional grabs, which can be very frustrating. This is why I decided to only support index grabbing - it's a concession to be sure, but one that seemed to lead to a better experience for most players.

In any case, I hope you enjoy the remaining puzzles! If you have any other feedback, feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com.

-Thomas

reviews.review_description: It's ok.

developer_response.body:
Hi delilahdeath, thank you for your review. 
Do you have any feedback on where you felt the game fell short or didn't meet your expectations? I'm always happy to learn, so feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com if you have any feedback!

reviews.review_description: This is the definition of mediocrity. The fact that a game has no bugs and works smoothly does not mean it is worth five stars...

developer_response.body:
Hi kaputis, thank you for your honest review and sorry you didn't have a better time with the game. 
If you have any feedback on how you feel the game could be improved, or if you feel any part of the trailer/description didn't set the right expectation for what the game was about, feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com!

reviews.review_description: Ok but overrated honestly.

developer_response.body: Hey Logica, thank you for your honesty and sorry to hear you didn’t have a better time with the game. If there were any aspects of it that didn’t meet your expectations based on what you had read/seen of the game prior, or that you felt could be improved - please feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com!

reviews.review_description: What is with all the 5 star reviews.  The game is ok.  I love puzzle games, and this game does work well, but 5 stars?  There’s nothing “wrong” with the game.  The simplistic design allows it to look fine graphically, and it seems to control just fine.  Before buying, I watched the video and felt I would be a little bored, but with all the 5 star reviews I figured I must be missing something - nope, it’s exactly as the video portrays.  Maybe I prefer my home video games to be story based.  For me, this is a puzzle game I’d have on my phone to play occasionally during long wait times (like at a doctors office).  So watch the video, if it all interests you - get it (or wait for a sale if you’re on the fence).

developer_response.body:
Hey ArmyWall, thank you for your honest review. I totally understand if this just isn't the type of game you enjoy coming back to. 
Feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com if you have any feedback on what you feel could be improved about the game!

reviews.review_description:
Very easy at first but then gets more challenging as you progress into the later stages that have you testing your spacial thinking. Great game to show people new to VR since the controls and concept are easy to grasp. 

Would really like to see more stages added in an update. Definitely worth pickng up if you like puzzlers.

I love games of this kind in RL, and this does a very good job being one in VR. The sets I found not too frustratingly difficult nor too easy, well-balanced. The graphics, well do what they should. There could be a whole lot more customization going on: opacity, size, textures, backgrounds, shapes etc. But OTOH the overall simplicity is alright as well, it does feel well-designed. I like the music very much, other people might like some variety as well. The controls are very much ok. 

The interface has the VERY annoying flaw to start just below your pelvis in sitting mode, otherwise it’s ok. 

The main flaw of the game is that it‘s almost the only dedicated real puzzle game as of now (Feb 2021), so it lacks comparison. But, it‘s good and can really be recommended.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review Ysohyps! 
I very much agree that there are far too few puzzle games on the store right now. Not much I can do about that personally, other than champion really good puzzle games that haven't gotten on to the store yet. If you haven't yet, I highly recommend Puzzling Places, which is available via App Lab.
Thank you for the feedback on the menu position as well - I added a way to reposition the menu in the settings, but that would obviously still be hard to reach if you're starting out seated. I'll look into adding a button shortcut for repositioning instead.

reviews.review_title: Baby Geniuses was a great movie!
reviews.review_description: Now i feel like one playing cubism, can be very challenging, needs a reward system and ability to change music. Otherwise it really gets my motor running with gas bees knees skidoo.

I'm only halfway through but i enjoy the concept very much. A good game for that chill out time before bed. But, it would be nice to to see the answers of previously solved puzzles. So we can redo them if we want.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review! 
By default, solved puzzles will spawn in the solved state when you re-open them. You can however check the "replay solved puzzles" checkbox in the settings if you want them to spawn unsolved to replay them. Hope that helps, and if you have any other feedback, feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com!

reviews.review_description: I played this while drunk and lost track of my existence. My entire reality became block. Fit block into hole. 10/10.

reviews.review_title: In dire need of quality fixes
reviews.review_description:
1) Found it super frustrating sitting to play comfortably despite recentering the guardian multiple times.  It's great that you can grab and move the puzzle but WHY not the menu.  Often I found the menu impossible to press as it would be below my legs or half inside my body.  Ideally just allow all elements of the game to be freely moved for comfort.

2) it's possible to throw shapes out of your own reach.  Distance of throw away from puzzle area should be capped.  Or better still, allow remote grabbing of the piece you are pointing to if your claw is not already intersecting it.

3) Music: Love the idea of isolated piano phrases for a chilled out game like this but the execution is poor.  Some piano samples stop too abruptly with no long decay of the final note and others do.  It's a bit jarring when the notes stop dead like they were cut prematurely in an audio editor.  For a chilled out game like this you should keep the long note decays it's more atmospheric.

These awkward aspects prevent this game from hitting it's true 5 star potential as a layer back Quest classic.

developer_response.body:
Update 5: Hey Rick, wanted to check in again to see if you've had a chance to try the game again since your review. Make sure to check out the advanced controls settings to easily handle far away pieces, the reposition shortcut on the thumbstick to easily reposition the menu, and the continuous music option in the audio settings to stop the music from cutting out when finishing a puzzle.
Hope these features help to give you a better experience than you initially had, and if not, feel free to share any additional feedback with me at thomas@cubism-vr.com.

Update 4: Hey Rick, I wanted to check in one more time - the game has come quite a long way in the last year, and with the latest update (v1.5.1) - repositioning the puzzle has become even more flexible. I remembered your comment on the difficulty of moving the menu, so wanted to let you know about these changes!

Update 3: patch 1.2.3 fixes an issue where the music would fade out when you only have one piece left, even though that piece doesn't fit into the puzzle. This may have contributed to some of the audio feeling unduly "cut off".

Update 2: update 1.2.0 makes it even easier to reposition the play area. Just press down on either thumb stick at any point.

Update 1: update 1.1.0 addresses your first issue by making it easier to move the entire play-area. See the 'reposition playarea' button in the settings for that. 

Hi RickK75, thank you for sharing your feedback. To address some of your points:

1. You can re-position the entire play-area including the menu by going to 'settings' > 'reposition height'. Although I'm currently working on a different update, I'm still planning on simplifying the UX of this in a future update.

2. In the settings, you can turn on 'advanced controls', which allows you to summon all loose pieces to your hand by pressing a. This can help when pieces are out of reach.

3. I think you may be referring to is the music fading out near the end of a puzzle. Whenever you place the second to last piece, the music fades out so you can hear the piano chord once you finish the puzzle. If you're hearing cut-off audio somewhere else in the game please reach out to me at the below email, and I'll be happy to try and address this issue. 

Feel free to contact me at thomas@cubism-vr.com if you have any other feedback!

reviews.review_description: Very polished game, but it could have been a lot better in terms of level design, seems basic unfortunately

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback.
I'm sorry to hear you were disappointed in the level design. Feel free to reach out to me directly at thomas@cubism-vr.com if you want to give me more detailed feedback on it!

reviews.review_description:
I’m a big puzzle game fan (loved Myst and The Room: A Dark Matter) and wanted another puzzle game. This one is straightforward - solve a bunch of 3D puzzles with Tetris-like pieces. Throw in the Chopin gently playing in the background (I love Chopin!) and this hits all the spots: challenging puzzles that make me think, relaxing, clean graphics, easy to play, hard to conquer. 
10/10 will play again.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review, MissMusicTeacher - given your username, I'm so glad you enjoyed the soundtrack!
The pianist (and music teacher herself!) who recorded these tracks for the game, Sahlia Wong, released the full soundtrack on Spotify and Bandcamp in case you want to listen to it outside of VR as well :)

reviews.review_description: Honestly, I was hesitant because of the fact that it cost money. But this is a great game for the price. The movements are very user friendly. Theres so many levels and it get pretty challenging!

This simple and satisfying interface makes for a snappy 3 dimensional puzzle building experience. It’s no frills approach makes it super responsive, and it’s exactly what you need, whether you realize it or not.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review, BobertB1!

reviews.review_title: VR Puzzle Game At Its Best
reviews.review_description:
Love this. The puzzle itself is beautiful in its simplicity and execution, and the use of hand-tracking is some of the best I've seen.

If you're into puzzles, this is a must-buy.

If you like puzzles, that is for you. Simple concept, low stress and very rewarding. I like how the difficulty slowly builds up. 
The environment is very calming (not much to see other then the object and lives you’re trying to put together).

At first I thought this game may be dull or tiresome, but that is definitely not the case. Some of the best things regarding this include: being able to lie in bed while playing, splendid integration with Oculus Quest 2 hardware APIs ( haptics, mechanics, etc) are all very exceptional and better than any other game I’ve played, definitely worth the $8.99. My grandmother also loves this game as well.
Best puzzle game yet!
3D puzzle is what you need in VR
I really love how immersive the game is. I feel like I’m in another world. It’s exactly what I was looking for.

My daughter and I play this and see who can do the hardest puzzles. Something else fun is to time each other on an easy puzzle to see who is fastest. That really adds a fun challenge to the game. 

My daughter has kids of her own, so we let them join the fun.  The cool thing is it is so easy to use my grand kids picked it up in minutes but game play is so challenging it can stump the adults sometimes. 

  This game is great I hope they explore adding patterns of famous buildings, fictional ones included. 


Caution this game is so fun you will not want to stop. We can get lost in the game, ops it is almost midnight...
